title: antennaria neglecta subsp. howellii
intro: Antennaria neglecta subsp. howellii, also known as Howell's Antennaria, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the western United States. It is a low-growing, spreading perennial with a history of traditional use in herbal medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits such as soothing inflammation, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. Antennaria neglecta subsp. howellii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice for herbalists and those seeking natural remedies.
